project_description:
Nuuk Clay Workshop

Final deliverable:
Operational Workshop

Collect Historical Shipping Data

Description:
Gather historical shipping delay data for Nuuk Harbour from 2018 to 2025, including ice blockage frequency, duration, and average customs clearance times. Use the Danish Maritime Authority (DMA) API to extract logs and compile a dataset for risk analysis.

Resources needed:
• Danish Maritime Authority API access
• Project Manager
• Supply Chain Coordinator
• Data analyst with Python skills

Run Risk Modeling Simulation

Description:
Use Pandas and Matplotlib in Python to analyze collected shipping data, map delay patterns by month, and simulate the probability of >4-week delays due to storms or ice. Validate results against Greenlandic Port Authority reports.

Resources needed:
• Python environment with Pandas and Matplotlib
• Data analyst
• Supply Chain Coordinator
• Project Manager

Validate with Arctic Logistics Expert

Description:
Engage a supply chain analyst specializing in Arctic logistics to review the risk model. Submit findings to the Danish Maritime Authority for cross-verification and present results to the Inuit Cultural Heritage Council for cultural context on shipping disruptions.

Resources needed:
• Arctic logistics expert
• Inuit Cultural Heritage Council representative
• Danish Maritime Authority contact
• Project Manager

Finalize Emergency Air Freight Plan

Description:
Simulate emergency air freight response time using Nordic Cargo’s transit data (72-hour max). Confirm integration capability with real-time Arctic shipping alerts and document contingency protocols for rapid activation.

Resources needed:
• Nordic Cargo contact
• IoT monitoring system integrator
• Supply Chain Coordinator
• Financial Analyst

Produce Final Risk Assessment Report

Description:
Compile all findings into a validated Arctic shipping risk assessment report by March 31, 2026. Include a 90% confidence interval for winter delay probabilities and recommendations for biannual import scheduling aligned with navigation windows.

Resources needed:
• Project Manager
• Supply Chain Coordinator
• Financial Analyst
• Legal Advisor (for contract alignment)

project_name: Conduct Winter Thermal Load Simulation Using EnergyPlus

project_description:
Run EnergyPlus Simulation

Description:
Conduct a dynamic thermal load simulation using EnergyPlus software with real-world boundary conditions, including Nuuk's extreme winter temperatures (-20°C), 90% occupancy levels, and peak kiln operation. Input verified Greenlandic meteorological data from the Danish Meteorological Institute to model temperature gradients, humidity, CO₂ buildup, and energy consumption across drying zones and workspaces. Validate results against physical sensor readings from a mock workshop setup in December 2025.

Resources needed:
• EnergyPlus software license
• Arctic-certified building specialist
• Historical climate data (Danish Meteorological Institute)
• Mock workshop setup with temporary heating systems
• Digital modeling technician

Validate Thermal Model Output

Description:
Perform statistical regression analysis to compare simulation outputs from EnergyPlus with real-time sensor data collected during the December 2025 mock test. Identify discrepancies in thermal stability, humidity control, and energy usage. Adjust input parameters as needed and confirm that all zones remain within safe operating parameters (±5°C variance) under 90% winter occupancy. Document findings for inclusion in the Winter Thermal Readiness Report.

Resources needed:
• Statistical analysis tools (Python/Pandas/Matplotlib)
• Data analyst
• IoT sensor data logs
• Simulation validation checklist
• Bilingual technical report template

Secure Expert Audit & Sign-Off

Description:
Engage an Arctic-certified building engineer to audit the EnergyPlus model and final thermal assessment. Review the simulation methodology, input assumptions, and output reliability. Obtain formal sign-off confirming the facility’s thermal readiness for year-round operation. Submit the signed report to the National Labour Inspectorate (Greenland) for compliance review and ensure alignment with NFPA and Bygningsreglementet standards.

Resources needed:
• Arctic-certified building engineer
• Compliance review checklist
• Signed audit confirmation form
• National Labour Inspectorate contact point
• Digital submission platform
